confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_5bcd00c2-a2d5-489a-82e6-79a1cf6d0db8 read back as promoted

evidence: Human file-drop intake and Staccato mailbox filtering both require rejecting candidate material safely while preserving why. Canonical Inbox currently stores admitted envelopes and promotion status, but the broader system needs a principled way to record rejected candidates and admission decisions before or around envelope creation.
proposal: Implement a Canonical Admission/Rejection Ledger with candidate id, source, observed_at, parser/filter used, decision, reason codes, evidence refs, deciding principal or system rule, authority level, resulting envelope id when admitted, and supersession/retry links. The ledger should cover file-drop candidates, mailbox message candidates, inbox promotions, outbox route validation, Site absorption candidates, and knowledge/task admission where applicable.
payload.recommendation: Start by using the ledger for human file-drop intake and mailbox participant-domain filtering. Dry-run should produce candidate decisions without mutation; admit should record durable decisions and create canonical envelopes only for admitted candidates.
payload.summary: Narada needs a canonical ledger for admission and rejection decisions. Rejection should not be represented by silence or disappearance. File drops, mailbox filters, proposals, Site absorption, routing choices, and task/knowledge promotions all need durable evidence for what was considered, admitted, rejected, deferred, or superseded.
payload.title: Canonical Admission and Rejection Ledger should be implemented

promotion.note: recorded_pending_crossing: executable promotion for target kind 'site_config_change' is not implemented yet.
